name: Global Multi-Region Ecommerce - 120k RPS Architecture Test

responsibility.notes: Synthetic global shopper traffic source for import testing.
Serves static assets and cacheable catalog pages before regional origin routing.
Weighted active-active regional routing with health-check based failaway.
Global event backbone fed by all active regions.

stateOwnership.transactionBoundary: Append-only order event stream keyed by orderId.

responsibility.notes: Aggregates order, payment, and catalog telemetry for near-real-time dashboards.
Immutable analytics sink for compliance, replay, and BI workloads.

stateOwnership.transactionBoundary: One immutable object batch per analytics window.

responsibility.notes: Sampled request and queue telemetry for the ecommerce architecture test.
US East ingress abuse and flash-sale surge limiter.
US East product-domain request router.

stateOwnership.transactionBoundary: One cart mutation per cartId and cartMutationId.

dataContracts.name: ShopperRequest
dataContracts.description: Inbound shopper request before regional routing. Used to model global traffic volume and public edge trust-boundary protection.

dataContracts.name: ShopperResponse
dataContracts.description: Generic synchronous response returned to the shopper-facing caller.

dataContracts.name: CatalogReadRequest
dataContracts.description: Product detail and listing request served from regional catalog cache and replicas.

dataContracts.name: CatalogReadResponse
dataContracts.description: Compact product result for catalog read requests.

dataContracts.name: SearchRequest
dataContracts.description: Search query routed to a regional search index.

dataContracts.name: SearchResponse
dataContracts.description: Search result metadata returned to clients.

dataContracts.name: CartMutationCommand
dataContracts.description: Idempotent add, remove, or quantity update for a shopper cart.

dataContracts.name: CartMutationResponse
dataContracts.description: Cart mutation acknowledgement.

dataContracts.name: CartState
dataContracts.description: Canonical regional cart state stored behind write-through cache.

dataContracts.name: CheckoutCommand
dataContracts.description: Idempotent checkout request that creates an order and starts asynchronous payment authorization.

dataContracts.name: OrderAcceptedResponse
dataContracts.description: Synchronous checkout acknowledgement returned after durable order acceptance.

dataContracts.name: InventoryReservationCommand
dataContracts.description: Inventory reservation requested during checkout.

dataContracts.name: InventoryRecord
dataContracts.description: Regional inventory availability and reservation counters.

dataContracts.name: OrderRecord
dataContracts.description: Durable regional order record. Order ids are globally unique so active-active regions can merge without duplicate primary keys.

dataContracts.name: OrderCreatedEvent
dataContracts.description: Append-only event emitted after an order record is committed.

dataContracts.name: PaymentAuthorizationCommand
dataContracts.description: Tokenized payment authorization command. Contains network token only, not raw card data.

dataContracts.name: PaymentAuthorizedEvent
dataContracts.description: Payment provider authorization result.

dataContracts.name: NotificationCommand
dataContracts.description: Order/payment notification command sent to regional realtime gateways.

dataContracts.name: ProductRecord
dataContracts.description: Product catalog projection served by caches, read replicas, and search indexes.

dataContracts.name: ProductIndexEvent
dataContracts.description: Catalog/search projection update broadcast from the global analytics plane.

dataContracts.name: AnalyticsEvent
dataContracts.description: Normalized analytics fact derived from regional order streams.

failureScenarios.name: Black Friday 3x global traffic spike
failureScenarios.expectedResponse: CDN absorbs cacheable reads, global traffic manager keeps weighted routing, queues absorb checkout/payment pressure.
failureScenarios.expectedUserImpact: Catalog and search should stay responsive; checkout may degrade if payment queues or providers saturate.
failureScenarios.recoveryBehavior: Autoscaled workers drain payment and notification queues after the spike ends.

failureScenarios.name: EU West region unavailable during peak checkout
failureScenarios.expectedResponse: Global traffic manager should stop sending new EU traffic and remaining regions should absorb part of the load.
failureScenarios.expectedUserImpact: EU shoppers may see errors during DNS/load-balancer failaway; already queued payment work is delayed until recovery.
failureScenarios.recoveryBehavior: EU services recover gradually and stream processors reconcile regional backlog into the global backbone.

failureScenarios.name: Payment providers become 5x slower
failureScenarios.expectedResponse: Circuit breakers and worker retry budgets prevent synchronous checkout from blocking on the provider.
failureScenarios.expectedUserImpact: Orders are accepted but payment confirmation notifications arrive late.
failureScenarios.recoveryBehavior: Payment workers drain durable queues after provider latency returns to normal.

failureScenarios.name: US order database primary failover
failureScenarios.expectedResponse: Order commits pause briefly while the regional database promotes a healthy primary.
failureScenarios.expectedUserImpact: Some US checkouts may be delayed or rejected; cart and catalog traffic should continue.
failureScenarios.recoveryBehavior: Order writes resume and regional order events catch up into payment and analytics streams.

failureScenarios.name: Global event backbone loses half capacity
failureScenarios.expectedResponse: Regional checkout remains independent while analytics and cross-region projections lag.
failureScenarios.expectedUserImpact: Customer-facing checkout should continue; dashboards and catalog/search projections may be stale.
failureScenarios.recoveryBehavior: Global stream processors process retained events and restore projection freshness.

assumptions.statement: Order ids are globally unique before any regional database write, so active-active regions can merge order records by orderId.
Payment providers honor paymentAttemptId as an idempotency key across retries and worker restarts.
The global traffic manager can remove an unhealthy region from routing within 60 seconds.

decisionRecords.title: Run checkout active-active across three regions
decisionRecords.context: Huge ecommerce peaks require regional capacity and lower latency for catalog, cart, and checkout traffic.
decisionRecords.decision: Use weighted active-active routing to US East, EU West, and AP Southeast with independent regional order writes and global event reconciliation.
alternatives: Single primary region with warm standby.
Centralized order database with all regions writing cross-region.
consequences: Order ids and payment attempts need strong idempotency.
Analytics and product projections are eventually consistent.
Region failaway must be tested explicitly.

decisionRecords.title: Accept orders before payment confirmation
decisionRecords.context: Payment providers have lower quota and higher latency than the internal checkout path during flash-sale traffic.
decisionRecords.decision: Commit the order, enqueue payment authorization, and notify the shopper asynchronously once payment completes.
alternatives: Reject checkout when payment provider is slow.
Use one global payment queue only.
consequences: Checkout response latency is protected from provider slowness.
Order state must expose pending payment status.
Payment queues and DLQs need operational replay ownership.
